_meta.purpose: The menu. Add, remove or edit dishes here. Nothing else needs changing.

you_write.name: the dish name
you_write.role: base | veg | meat | fish | soup | dessert — which slot of the meal it fills
you_write.cuisine: singaporean | japanese | western — used by the cuisine filter
you_write.appliances: every appliance this dish can be cooked on, e.g. ['stovetop','pressure_cooker']
you_write.ingredients: plain strings, e.g. '800 g pork ribs'
you_write.steps: the method, in order. Say how long things cook — 'simmer for 1 hour'.
you_write.steps_variants: optional. Different steps for a given appliance. Times stated here belong to that appliance.
you_write.handson_action: how the active cooking reads in the timeline, e.g. 'stir-frying over high heat'
you_write.serves: how many the stated quantities feed
you_write.base_volume: litres in the pot at serves-size; drives batching on small appliances
you_write.fresh: true if it must be served straight away
you_write.oven_temp_c: baking temperature; two oven dishes may share the oven if within 10C
you_write.cuts: optional cut choices, each with its own ingredients and steps
you_write.swaps: optional protein swaps, same shape as cuts
you_write.pack: optional shortcut using a ready-made paste
you_write.needs: optional. A prerequisite the cook must have ready, e.g. 'cooked rice'. Shown as a note on the dish.

_meta.derived_automatically: cooking time for every appliance, what the cooking produces, the shopping list and its quantities, shopping aisles, the taste profile, the protein tag, slow-versus-quick cooking, and how each ingredient is prepped. Never write these by hand.
_meta.overrides: If you disagree with the engine, add an explicit 'time' or 'results' to a dish and it wins.

dishes.name: Baked Chicken
steps: Preheat the oven to 180°C.
Put the chicken in a baking pan or casserole dish.
Sprinkle with garlic powder and pepper to taste.
Bake for 1 hour.

air_fryer: Season the chicken.
Air-fry at 180 C for 18 minutes, flipping halfway, until golden and cooked through.

dishes.name: Baked Lemon Chicken
ingredients: 1600 g whole chicken, skinned and cut into 10 pieces
steps: Combine salt, pepper, garlic, and thyme.
Lay chicken pieces into a baking pan.
Sprinkle seasonings over chicken.
Combine onions, stock, and lemon juice in a sauce pan.
Heat to a boil.
Pour hot lemon mixture around chicken.
Top each piece with a lemon slice.
Bake for 30 minutes at 200°C until golden brown.

air_fryer: Season the chicken and squeeze over the lemon.
Air-fry at 200 C for 18 minutes, flipping halfway, until golden and cooked through.

dishes.name: Stir Fry Vegetables and Beef
steps: Mix spices, soy sauce, and water; set aside.
Chop broccoli, peppers, and onions.
Heat oil in a large frying pan and add meat when oil is hot; stir until brown.
Add carrots, onions, and peppers.
Cook for one minute.
Add mushrooms and broccoli.
Add liquid mixture and cook until bubbly.
Reduce heat, cover pan, and cook two more minutes.
Serve over brown rice.

dishes.name: Crispy Oven-Fried Chicken
steps: Preheat oven to 180°C.
Add poultry seasoning to milk.
Combine remaining spices with cornflake crumbs in a plastic bag.
Dip chicken into milk, then shake in the bag with the crumbs.
Refrigerate for 1 hour.
Sprinkle lightly with paprika.
Space chicken on a greased baking pan.
Cover with foil and bake for 40 minutes.
Remove foil and bake for another 30 to 40 minutes until crispy.

air_fryer: Coat the chicken in the seasoned crumb.
Air-fry at 190 C for 18 minutes, flipping halfway, until crisp and golden.

dishes.name: Baked Fish
steps: Preheat oven to 180°C.
Place the fillets on a piece of foil.
Spread the onions, salt, pepper, and oil on top.
Fold the foil over the fish to wrap it.
Place on a baking sheet and bake for 15 to 20 minutes until the fish is flaky.

air_fryer: Season the fish.
Air-fry at 180 C for 10 minutes until just flaky.

dishes.name: Beef & Vegetable Soup
steps: In a large pan, brown ground beef over medium heat for 8 to 10 minutes.
Drain off fat.
Add corn, carrots, potatoes, tomatoes, and onions to pot.
Cook for 25 minutes over medium heat.
Add macaroni.
Cook for 20 minutes over medium heat.

pressure_cooker: Using the saute setting, brown the ground beef for 8 to 10 minutes, then drain off the fat.
Add the corn, carrots, potatoes, tomatoes, and onions with the water.
Seal and pressure-cook on high for 10 minutes, then quick-release.
Stir in the macaroni and pressure-cook for another 4 minutes, then quick-release.
slow_cooker: Brown the ground beef in a pan for 8 to 10 minutes, then drain and transfer to the slow cooker.
Add the corn, carrots, potatoes, tomatoes, and onions with the water.
Cover and cook on low for 6 hours.
Stir in the macaroni for the last 30 minutes.

dishes.name: Chicken Stew
steps: Remove the skin from the chicken.
Combine chicken, water, garlic, onion, salt, pepper, tomatoes, and parsley in a large pot.
Tightly cover and cook over low heat for 25 minutes.
Add celery, potatoes, carrots, and bay leaves and continue to cook for 15 minutes.
Remove bay leaves before serving.

pressure_cooker: Remove the skin from the chicken.
Combine the chicken, water, garlic, onion, salt, pepper, tomatoes, celery, potatoes, carrots, and bay leaves in the pressure cooker.
Seal and pressure-cook on high for 15 minutes, then quick-release.
Remove the bay leaves before serving.
slow_cooker: Remove the skin from the chicken.
Combine all the ingredients in the slow cooker.
Cover and cook on low for 5 to 6 hours until tender.
Remove the bay leaves before serving.

dishes.name: 3-Can Chili
steps: Place the contents of all 3 cans into a pan.
Add chili powder to taste.
Stir to mix.
Continue to stir over medium heat until heated thoroughly.

pressure_cooker: Add the contents of all 3 cans to the pressure cooker with the chili powder.
Seal and pressure-cook on high for 5 minutes, then quick-release.
Stir before serving.
slow_cooker: Add the contents of all 3 cans to the slow cooker with the chili powder.
Cover and cook on low for 3 to 4 hours.

dishes.name: Slow Cooker Lentil Soup
steps: Mix all ingredients together in slow cooker.
Cook on low for 8 to 10 hours.
Serve hot with crackers or bread.

pressure_cooker: Add all the ingredients to the pressure cooker.
Seal and pressure-cook on high for 18 minutes.
Let the pressure release naturally for 10 minutes.
Serve hot with crackers or bread.

dishes.name: Cabbage Salad
steps: Slice all vegetables thinly and put in a large bowl.
Add dill to taste.
Mix mayonnaise, yogurt, mustard, lemon juice, and sugar in a small bowl.
Blend well.
Add to vegetables and mix well.
Keep refrigerated until ready to eat.

dishes.name: Black Bean & Couscous Salad
steps: Bring broth to a boil in a small pot, and stir in couscous.
Cover the pot and remove from the heat.
Let stand for 5 minutes.
In a large bowl, whisk together oil, vinegar, cumin, and lime juice.
Add beans, onion, pepper, and corn, and toss.
Fluff the couscous with a fork.
Add to the beans and vegetables and mix well.
dishes.handson_action: boiling the broth, then tossing it together

dishes.name: 5 A Day Salad
steps: Wash all of the vegetables and mix them together in a large bowl.
Top with the low-fat dressing of your choice.

dishes.name: Apple Oatmeal Muffins
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C.
In a mixing bowl, add milk and applesauce.
Stir until blended.
Stir in flour, oats, sugar, baking powder, and cinnamon.
Mix until moistened, do not over mix.
Gently stir in the chopped apples.
Spoon into cupcake holders.
Bake for 15 to 20 minutes.

dishes.name: Angel Food Cake
steps: Preheat the oven to 160°C.
In a large mixing bowl, beat the egg whites until foamy.
Add the cream of tartar and beat until stiff.
Gradually beat in the sugar.
Add the vanilla and salt and gently fold in the flour.
Pour into an angel food pan and bake for 1 hour and 15 minutes, until the top is nicely browned.

dishes.name: Steamed Jasmine Rice
steps: Rinse the rice until the water runs clear.
Add the rice and water to a pot.
Bring to a boil, then cover and cook on low heat for 15 minutes.
Turn off the heat and let it steam, covered, for 10 minutes.
Fluff with a fork.

rice_cooker: Rinse the rice until the water runs clear.
Add the rice and water to the rice cooker and close the lid.
Press the cook button and leave it.
When it switches to keep-warm, let it steam for 10 minutes, then fluff with a fork.
oven: Rinse the rice and put it in an oven dish with the water.
Rest, covered, for 5 minutes, then fluff with a fork.

dishes.name: Garlic Stir-Fried Kailan
steps: Blanch the kailan in boiling water for 1 minute and drain.
Heat oil in a wok and stir-fry the garlic until fragrant.
Add the kailan and oyster sauce with a splash of water.
Stir-fry over high heat for 2 minutes until tender-crisp.

dishes.name: Sambal Long Beans
ingredients: 300 g long beans, cut into lengths
steps: Heat oil and stir-fry the shallots, garlic, and dried shrimp until fragrant.
Add the sambal and fry for one minute.
Add the long beans and sugar.
Stir-fry over medium heat for 6 to 8 minutes until the beans are tender.

dishes.name: Stir-Fried Bok Choy with Mushroom
steps: Heat oil and stir-fry the garlic and mushrooms until softened.
Add the bok choy and oyster sauce.
Stir-fry over high heat for 3 minutes until the greens wilt.
Finish with sesame oil.

dishes.name: Hainanese Poached Chicken
steps: Blanch the whole chicken in boiling water for 30 seconds, then lift out.
Place the chicken in a pot with ginger, spring onion, and salt, and add water to just cover.
Poach on the lowest heat for 45 minutes, keeping the water barely trembling.
Turn off the heat and let the chicken rest in the broth for 10 minutes.
Brush with sesame oil before slicing.

dishes.name: Char Siu Oven Pork
steps: Mix the hoisin, soy sauce, half the honey, five spice, garlic, and sugar into a marinade.
Coat the pork and marinate overnight in the fridge.
Roast at 200 C for 25 minutes.
Brush with the remaining honey and roast for another 15 minutes until caramelised and sticky.
Rest for 10 minutes before slicing.

air_fryer: Mix the hoisin, soy sauce, half the honey, five spice, garlic, and sugar into a marinade.
Coat the pork and marinate overnight in the fridge.
Air-fry at 200 C for 20 minutes, brushing with the remaining honey and flipping halfway, until caramelised and sticky.
Rest for 10 minutes before slicing.

dishes.name: Minced Pork with Tofu
steps: Brown the minced pork in a hot pan until it changes colour.
Add the garlic and ginger and fry until fragrant.
Add soy sauce, oyster sauce, and water, and bring to a simmer.
Gently add the tofu and simmer for 5 minutes.
Stir in the cornstarch slurry to thicken, and scatter with spring onion.

dishes.name: Steamed Fish with Ginger and Scallion
steps: Lay half the ginger under the fish on a plate and the rest on top.
Steam over high heat for 8 to 10 minutes until the fish is just flaky.
Scatter the spring onion over the fish.
Heat the oil until smoking and pour it over the fish, then drizzle with soy sauce and sesame oil.

dishes.name: Sweet & Sour Fish
steps: Coat the fish in cornstarch.
Deep-fry for 3 minutes until golden and crisp, then drain.
In another pan, simmer the ketchup, sugar, and vinegar into a sauce.
Add the pepper, onion, and pineapple and cook for 2 minutes.
Toss the fried fish in the sauce to coat.

fish.label: Fish
fish.name: Sweet & Sour Fish
steps: Coat the fish in cornstarch.
Deep-fry for 3 minutes until golden and crisp, then drain.
In another pan, simmer the ketchup, sugar, and vinegar into a sauce.
Add the pepper, onion, and pineapple and cook for 2 minutes.
Toss the fried fish in the sauce to coat.

chicken.label: Chicken
chicken.name: Sweet & Sour Chicken
steps: Coat the chicken in cornstarch.
Deep-fry for 6 minutes until golden and cooked through, then drain.
In another pan, simmer the ketchup, sugar, and vinegar into a sauce.
Add the pepper, onion, and pineapple and cook for 2 minutes.
Toss the fried chicken in the sauce to coat.

pork.label: Pork
pork.name: Sweet & Sour Pork
steps: Coat the pork in cornstarch.
Deep-fry for 7 minutes until golden and cooked through, then drain.
In another pan, simmer the ketchup, sugar, and vinegar into a sauce.
Add the pepper, onion, and pineapple and cook for 2 minutes.
Toss the fried pork in the sauce to coat.

dishes.name: ABC Soup
steps: Blanch the pork ribs in boiling water for 2 minutes, then drain and rinse.
Add the ribs, carrots, potatoes, tomatoes, onion, and corn to a pot with the water.
Bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat and simmer gently for 90 minutes until the meat is tender and the broth is sweet.
Season with salt.

pressure_cooker: Blanch the pork ribs in boiling water for 2 minutes, then drain and rinse.
Add the ribs, carrots, potatoes, tomatoes, onion, and corn to the pressure cooker with the water.
Seal the lid and pressure-cook on high for 30 minutes.
Let the pressure release naturally for 10 minutes, then quick-release the rest.
Season with salt.
slow_cooker: Blanch the pork ribs in boiling water for 2 minutes, then drain and rinse.
Add the ribs, carrots, potatoes, tomatoes, onion, corn, and water to the slow cooker.
Cover and cook on low for 6 to 8 hours until the meat is tender.

dishes.name: Watercress Soup
steps: Blanch the pork ribs in boiling water for 2 minutes, then drain.
Add the ribs, ginger, and water to a pot and bring to a boil.
Simmer gently for 60 minutes.
Add the watercress and simmer for another 30 minutes.
Season with salt.

pressure_cooker: Blanch the pork ribs in boiling water for 2 minutes, then drain.
Add the ribs, ginger, watercress, and water to the pressure cooker.
Seal and pressure-cook on high for 25 minutes, then let the pressure release naturally.
Season with salt.
slow_cooker: Blanch the pork ribs in boiling water for 2 minutes, then drain.
Add the ribs, ginger, watercress, and water to the slow cooker.
Cover and cook on low for 5 to 6 hours.

dishes.name: Bak Kut Teh
steps: Blanch the pork ribs in boiling water for 2 minutes, then drain.
Lightly crush the garlic and toast the white peppercorns, star anise, cinnamon, cloves, and dang gui to release their aroma.
Add the ribs, garlic, and toasted spices to a pot with the water and soy sauce.
Bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat and simmer gently for 90 minutes until the pork is tender.
Season with salt.

pack.label: Bak kut teh spice pack
steps: Blanch the pork ribs in boiling water for 2 minutes, then drain.
Add the ribs, crushed garlic, and the bak kut teh spice pack to a pot with the water and soy sauce.
Bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat and simmer gently for 90 minutes until the pork is tender.
Season with salt.

dishes.name: Chicken Curry
steps: Blend the shallots, garlic, ginger, lemongrass, dried chillies, turmeric, coriander, and cumin into a paste.
Heat oil and fry the paste with the curry leaves until fragrant and the oil splits.
Add the chicken and brown for 5 minutes.
Pour in the coconut milk and add the potatoes.
Simmer for 30 minutes until the chicken is tender.
Season with salt.

pack.label: Chicken curry paste
steps: Heat oil and fry the chicken curry paste with the curry leaves until fragrant.
Add the chicken and brown for 5 minutes.
Pour in the coconut milk and add the potatoes.
Simmer for 30 minutes until the chicken is tender.
Season with salt.

dishes.name: Fish Curry
steps: Blend the shallots, garlic, dried chillies, turmeric, and belacan into a paste.
Heat oil and fry the paste with the curry leaves until fragrant.
Add the tamarind, coconut milk, okra, and tomatoes, and simmer into a gravy for 10 minutes.
Slide in the fish and simmer gently for 8 minutes until just cooked.
Season with salt.

pack.label: Fish curry paste
steps: Heat oil and fry the fish curry paste with the curry leaves until fragrant.
Add the tamarind, coconut milk, okra, and tomatoes, and simmer into a gravy for 10 minutes.
Slide in the fish and simmer gently for 8 minutes until just cooked.
Season with salt.

dishes.name: Mutton Curry
steps: Blend the shallots, garlic, ginger, dried chillies, coriander, cumin, and fennel into a paste.
Heat oil and fry the paste with the cinnamon and cardamom until fragrant.
Add the mutton and brown for 8 minutes.
Pour in the coconut milk and add the potatoes.
Braise for 90 minutes until the mutton is fall-apart tender.
Season with salt.
dishes.handson_action: searing, then setting it to braise

pack.label: Meat curry paste
steps: Heat oil and fry the meat curry paste until fragrant.
Add the mutton and brown for 8 minutes.
Pour in the coconut milk and add the potatoes.
Braise for 90 minutes until the mutton is fall-apart tender.
Season with salt.

dishes.name: Beef Curry
steps: Blend the shallots, garlic, ginger, lemongrass, galangal, dried chillies, and coriander into a paste.
Heat oil and fry the paste until fragrant and the oil splits.
Add the beef and brown for 8 minutes.
Pour in the coconut milk.
Braise for 90 minutes until the beef is tender and the gravy thickens.
Season with salt.
dishes.handson_action: searing, then setting it to braise

pack.label: Rendang paste
steps: Heat oil and fry the rendang paste until fragrant.
Add the beef and brown for 8 minutes.
Pour in the coconut milk.
Braise for 90 minutes until the beef is tender and the gravy thickens.
Season with salt.

dishes.name: Fried Rice
steps: Heat oil and scramble the eggs, then set aside.
Fry the garlic, onion, and carrot until fragrant.
Add the cold rice and break up the clumps.
Stir-fry over high heat, tossing, for 4 minutes.
Add the peas, soy sauce, white pepper, and the eggs.
Toss until everything is coated and hot.
Scatter with spring onion.

dishes.name: Mee Goreng
steps: Heat oil and fry the garlic and shallots until fragrant.
Add the prawns and cook until pink.
Push aside and scramble the eggs.
Add the noodles, kecap manis, and sambal, and toss over high heat.
Add the bean sprouts, tomato, and potato.
Stir-fry for 3 minutes until everything is coated and hot.

dishes.name: Sambal Kangkong
steps: Heat oil and fry the garlic, shallots, dried shrimp, and belacan until fragrant.
Add the sambal and fry for one minute.
Add the kangkong and stir-fry over high heat for 2 minutes until just wilted.

dishes.name: Black Pepper Beef
steps: Sear the beef in hot oil for 5 minutes until browned, then set aside.
Fry the garlic, onion, and bell pepper until fragrant.
Add the crushed black pepper, oyster sauce, and soy sauce, and sizzle.
Return the beef, add the butter, and toss until glossy and coated.

beef.label: Beef
beef.name: Black Pepper Beef
steps: Sear the beef in hot oil for 5 minutes until browned, then set aside.
Fry the garlic, onion, and bell pepper until fragrant.
Add the crushed black pepper, oyster sauce, and soy sauce, and sizzle.
Return the beef, add the butter, and toss until glossy and coated.

chicken.label: Chicken
chicken.name: Black Pepper Chicken
steps: Sear the chicken in hot oil for 6 minutes until browned and cooked through, then set aside.
Fry the garlic, onion, and bell pepper until fragrant.
Add the crushed black pepper, oyster sauce, and soy sauce, and sizzle.
Return the chicken, add the butter, and toss until glossy and coated.

pork.label: Pork
pork.name: Black Pepper Pork
steps: Sear the pork in hot oil for 6 minutes until browned and cooked through, then set aside.
Fry the garlic, onion, and bell pepper until fragrant.
Add the crushed black pepper, oyster sauce, and soy sauce, and sizzle.
Return the pork, add the butter, and toss until glossy and coated.

dishes.name: Salted Egg Chicken
steps: Coat the chicken in cornstarch.
Deep-fry the chicken for 6 minutes until golden and crisp, then drain.
Melt the butter and fry the curry leaves and chilli until fragrant.
Add the mashed salted egg yolks and stir until foamy.
Pour in the evaporated milk to make a creamy sauce.
Toss the fried chicken in the sauce to coat.

chicken.label: Chicken
chicken.name: Salted Egg Chicken
steps: Coat the chicken in cornstarch.
Deep-fry the chicken for 6 minutes until golden and crisp, then drain.
Melt the butter and fry the curry leaves and chilli until fragrant.
Add the mashed salted egg yolks and stir until foamy.
Pour in the evaporated milk to make a creamy sauce.
Toss the fried chicken in the sauce to coat.

prawn.label: Prawn
prawn.name: Salted Egg Prawns
steps: Coat the prawn in cornstarch.
Deep-fry the prawn for 3 minutes until golden and crisp, then drain.
Melt the butter and fry the curry leaves and chilli until fragrant.
Add the mashed salted egg yolks and stir until foamy.
Pour in the evaporated milk to make a creamy sauce.
Toss the fried prawn in the sauce to coat.

fish.label: Fish
fish.name: Salted Egg Fish
steps: Coat the fish in cornstarch.
Deep-fry the fish for 3 minutes until golden and crisp, then drain.
Melt the butter and fry the curry leaves and chilli until fragrant.
Add the mashed salted egg yolks and stir until foamy.
Pour in the evaporated milk to make a creamy sauce.
Toss the fried fish in the sauce to coat.

dishes.name: Tomato and Egg Stir-Fry
steps: Beat the eggs with a pinch of salt.
Heat oil and scramble the eggs until just set, then set aside.
Stir-fry the garlic and tomatoes over high heat until softened and juicy.
Add the soy sauce and sugar.
Return the eggs and toss gently to combine.
Scatter with spring onion.

dishes.name: Chinese Steamed Egg
steps: Beat the eggs with the water and salt until smooth, then strain.
Pour into a shallow dish.
Steam over low heat for 12 minutes until just set and silky.
Drizzle with soy sauce and sesame oil, and scatter with spring onion.

dishes.name: Oyster Omelette
steps: Mix the cornstarch with the water into a batter.
Heat oil and pan-fry the batter into a crispy pancake.
Crack the eggs over the top and let them set.
Add the oysters and garlic, and pan-fry until the oysters are just cooked and the edges are crisp.
Season with fish sauce.
Scatter with spring onion and chilli.

dishes.name: Egg Foo Young
steps: Beat the eggs with salt.
Stir in the prawns, bean sprouts, garlic, and spring onion.
Heat oil and pour in the egg mixture.
Pan-fry until golden and set on both sides.
Serve with soy sauce.

dishes.name: Bubur Cha Cha
steps: Steam the sweet potato and yam cubes until tender.
Boil the sago until translucent, then rinse.
Simmer the coconut milk with the gula melaka, pandan, and salt until the sugar dissolves.
Add the sweet potato, yam, and sago, and simmer gently for 5 minutes.
Serve warm or chilled.

dishes.name: Tau Suan
steps: Soak and steam the mung beans until just tender but still whole.
Bring the water to a boil with the pandan and sugar.
Stir in the sweet potato starch slurry until the soup thickens to a gel.
Add the mung beans and simmer gently for 2 minutes.
Serve warm.

dishes.name: Red Bean Soup
steps: Rinse the red beans.
Bring the beans, pandan, orange peel, and water to a boil.
Reduce the heat and simmer for 60 minutes until the beans are soft and breaking down.
Add the sugar and simmer for another 10 minutes until sweet and slightly thickened.
Serve warm.
dishes.handson_action: stirring as it simmers and thickens

dishes.name: Pulut Hitam
steps: Soak the black glutinous rice overnight.
Bring the rice, pandan, and water to a boil, then simmer for 45 minutes until the grains burst and thicken.
Stir in the gula melaka and salt until dissolved.
Serve warm, drizzled with coconut milk.
dishes.handson_action: stirring as it simmers and thickens

dishes.name: Chicken Baked Rice
steps: Heat oil and stir-fry the garlic, onion, and chicken until the chicken is cooked.
Add the ketchup and tomato and simmer into a sauce.
Spread the rice in a baking dish and pour the chicken sauce over.
Top with the grated cheese.
Bake at 200 C for 15 minutes until the cheese is golden and bubbling.

air_fryer: Stir-fry the garlic, onion, and chicken until cooked, then simmer with the ketchup and tomato into a sauce.
Spread the rice in an air-fryer dish, pour over the sauce, and top with cheese.
Air-fry at 200 C for 10 minutes until the cheese is golden and bubbling.

dishes.name: Seafood Baked Rice
steps: Melt the butter and stir-fry the garlic, onion, prawns, and squid until just cooked.
Stir in the cream to make a sauce.
Spread the rice in a baking dish and pour the seafood sauce over.
Top with the grated cheese.
Bake at 200 C for 15 minutes until golden and bubbling.

air_fryer: Stir-fry the garlic, onion, prawns, and squid in butter, then stir in the cream to make a sauce.
Spread the rice in an air-fryer dish, pour over the sauce, and top with cheese.
Air-fry at 200 C for 10 minutes until golden and bubbling.

dishes.name: Chicken Katsu
steps: Season the chicken and dredge each piece in flour, then egg, then panko.
Deep-fry the chicken for 6 minutes until golden and crisp, then drain and rest.
Slice and serve over shredded cabbage with tonkatsu sauce.

air_fryer: Coat the chicken in flour, egg, and panko.
Air-fry the chicken at 200 C for 12 minutes, flipping halfway, until golden and crisp.
Slice and serve over shredded cabbage with tonkatsu sauce.

dishes.name: Salmon Teriyaki
steps: Mix the soy sauce, mirin, sake, and sugar into a teriyaki sauce.
Pan-fry the salmon skin-side down until crisp, then flip and cook through, about 6 minutes.
Pour in the teriyaki sauce and cook until glossy and thickened, coating the salmon.
Scatter with scallion.

dishes.name: Gyudon
steps: Simmer the dashi, soy sauce, mirin, and sugar with the sliced onion for 5 minutes until the onion softens.
Add the thinly sliced beef and simmer for 3 minutes until just cooked.
Spoon the beef and onion over bowls of rice and drizzle with the broth.
Top with scallion.

dishes.name: Miso Soup
steps: Heat the dashi until warm, about 5 minutes.
Whisk in the miso paste until dissolved.
Add the cubed tofu and wakame and heat through gently without boiling.
Scatter with scallion.

dishes.name: Agedashi Tofu
steps: Cut the tofu into cubes and coat in cornstarch.
Deep-fry the tofu for 4 minutes until golden and crisp, then drain.
Simmer the dashi, soy sauce, and mirin into a light broth for 3 minutes.
Place the fried tofu in bowls, pour over the broth, and top with grated daikon and scallion.

dishes.name: Yakisoba
steps: Heat oil and stir-fry the pork until browned.
Add the onion, carrot, and cabbage and stir-fry until softened.
Add the noodles and bean sprouts.
Pour in the tonkatsu sauce and toss over high heat until everything is coated and glossy.
Top with nori and scallion.

dishes.name: Ramen
steps: Simmer the stock with the miso and soy sauce for 15 minutes to build the broth.
Boil the eggs for 7 minutes, then peel and halve.
Cook the ramen noodles until just tender, then drain.
Slice the pork belly.
Divide the noodles into bowls, pour over the hot broth, and top with pork, egg, bean sprouts, nori, and scallion.

dishes.name: Katsudon
steps: Bread the pork in flour, egg, and panko.
Deep-fry the pork for 6 minutes until golden and crisp, then slice.
Simmer the dashi, soy sauce, mirin, and sugar with the sliced onion for 5 minutes.
Lay the sliced cutlet in the broth, pour the beaten eggs over the top, and cook until just set.
Slide over bowls of rice and top with scallion.

dishes.name: Japanese Beef Curry
steps: Brown the beef, onion, carrot, and potato in oil.
Add the water and simmer for 20 minutes until the vegetables are tender.
Stir in the curry roux until dissolved and the sauce thickens.
Simmer for 5 more minutes, then serve.

dishes.name: Soy-Braised Pork Ribs
steps: Sear the pork ribs in oil until browned on all sides.
Stir in the soy sauce, dark soy sauce, sugar, garlic, ginger, star anise, and water.
Cover and braise gently for 50 minutes until the meat is fall-apart tender.
Spoon the glossy sauce over the pork to serve.
dishes.handson_action: searing, then setting it to braise

ribs.label: Ribs
ribs.name: Soy-Braised Pork Ribs
steps: Sear the pork ribs in oil until browned on all sides.
Stir in the soy sauce, dark soy sauce, sugar, garlic, ginger, star anise, and water.
Cover and braise gently for 50 minutes until the meat is fall-apart tender.
Spoon the glossy sauce over the pork to serve.
ribs.handson_action: searing, then setting it to braise
ribs.note: A collagen-rich cut — braised low and slow until fall-apart tender.

belly.label: Belly
belly.name: Soy-Braised Pork Belly
steps: Sear the pork belly in oil until browned on all sides.
Stir in the soy sauce, dark soy sauce, sugar, garlic, ginger, star anise, and water.
Cover and braise gently for 50 minutes until the meat is fall-apart tender.
Spoon the glossy sauce over the pork to serve.
belly.handson_action: searing, then setting it to braise
belly.note: A fatty, collagen-rich cut — braised until meltingly tender.

shoulder.label: Shoulder
shoulder.name: Soy-Braised Pork Shoulder
steps: Sear the pork shoulder in oil until browned on all sides.
Stir in the soy sauce, dark soy sauce, sugar, garlic, ginger, star anise, and water.
Cover and braise gently for 50 minutes until the meat is fall-apart tender.
Spoon the glossy sauce over the pork to serve.
shoulder.handson_action: searing, then setting it to braise
shoulder.note: A collagen-rich cut — braised low and slow until it shreds.

loin.label: Loin (lean)
loin.name: Soy-Braised Pork Loin
steps: Sear the pork loin in oil for three minutes each side until browned.
Stir in the soy sauce, sugar, garlic, and ginger and cook until glossy, about two minutes.
Rest briefly, then slice — a lean cut, seared quickly rather than braised so it stays juicy.
loin.note: A lean cut — seared quickly so it stays juicy, not braised.

dishes.name: Soy-Braised Beef Chuck
steps: Sear the beef chuck in oil until browned on all sides.
Stir in the soy sauce, dark soy sauce, sugar, garlic, ginger, star anise, and water.
Cover and braise gently for 50 minutes until the meat is fall-apart tender.
Spoon the glossy sauce over to serve.
dishes.handson_action: searing, then setting it to braise

chuck.label: Chuck
chuck.note: A collagen-rich cut — braised low and slow until it pulls apart.
chuck.name: Soy-Braised Beef Chuck
steps: Sear the beef chuck in oil until browned on all sides.
Stir in the soy sauce, dark soy sauce, sugar, garlic, ginger, star anise, and water.
Cover and braise gently for 50 minutes until the meat is fall-apart tender.
Spoon the glossy sauce over to serve.
chuck.handson_action: searing, then setting it to braise

brisket.label: Brisket
brisket.note: A collagen-rich cut — braised low and slow until it pulls apart.
brisket.name: Soy-Braised Beef Brisket
steps: Sear the beef brisket in oil until browned on all sides.
Stir in the soy sauce, dark soy sauce, sugar, garlic, ginger, star anise, and water.
Cover and braise gently for 50 minutes until the meat is fall-apart tender.
Spoon the glossy sauce over to serve.
brisket.handson_action: searing, then setting it to braise

short_rib.label: Short rib
short_rib.note: A collagen-rich cut — braised low and slow until it pulls apart.
short_rib.name: Soy-Braised Beef Short Rib
steps: Sear the beef short rib in oil until browned on all sides.
Stir in the soy sauce, dark soy sauce, sugar, garlic, ginger, star anise, and water.
Cover and braise gently for 50 minutes until the meat is fall-apart tender.
Spoon the glossy sauce over to serve.
short_rib.handson_action: searing, then setting it to braise

sirloin.label: Sirloin (lean)
sirloin.note: A lean cut — seared fast and rested; braising it would turn it grey and dry.
sirloin.name: Soy-Seared Beef Sirloin
steps: Sear the beef sirloin in oil for two minutes each side for medium-rare, browning the surface.
Stir in the soy sauce, sugar, garlic, and ginger and glaze for one minute.
Rest, then slice against the grain — a lean cut, seared fast so it stays tender.

dishes.name: Soy-Braised Chicken Thigh
steps: Sear the chicken thigh skin-side down until golden.
Stir in the soy sauce, dark soy sauce, sugar, garlic, ginger, and water.
Cover and braise gently for 25 minutes until tender and glossy.
Spoon the sauce over to serve.
dishes.handson_action: searing, then setting it to braise

thigh.label: Thigh
thigh.note: The fattier, forgiving cut — stays moist through a braise or a fry.
thigh.name: Soy-Braised Chicken Thigh
steps: Sear the chicken thigh skin-side down until golden.
Stir in the soy sauce, dark soy sauce, sugar, garlic, ginger, and water.
Cover and braise gently for 25 minutes until tender and glossy.
Spoon the sauce over to serve.
thigh.handson_action: searing, then setting it to braise

breast.label: Breast (lean)
breast.note: Lean and quick to dry — cooked briefly and gently, never braised hard.
breast.name: Soy-Glazed Chicken Breast
steps: Sear the chicken breast briefly on each side.
Stir in the soy sauce, sugar, garlic, and ginger.
Cook gently for eight minutes, just until cooked through — a lean cut, cooked quick and gentle so it stays juicy, not braised.
Rest, then slice.

dishes.name: Spinach Goma-ae
steps: Blanch the spinach in boiling water for one minute, then drain and squeeze out the water.
Pan-fry the sesame seeds in a dry pan for two minutes until fragrant, then grind them to a coarse paste.
Whisk the soy sauce, mirin, and sugar into the ground sesame, then toss the spinach through the dressing.

dishes.name: Cucumber Sunomono
steps: Slice the cucumbers paper-thin, salt them lightly, and squeeze out the water.
Rehydrate the wakame in cold water, then drain.
Whisk the rice vinegar, sugar, and salt together, then toss with the cucumber and wakame and scatter with sesame seeds.

dishes.name: Purin
steps: Simmer half the sugar with a splash of water until it turns amber, then pour the caramel into four ramekins.
Whisk the eggs, milk, remaining sugar, and vanilla into a smooth custard, then strain it.
Bake the custards in a water bath at 160 C for 40 minutes, until just set.
Refrigerate for 2 hours before turning out.
dishes.handson_action: making caramel, then into the oven

dishes.name: Matcha Warabi Mochi
steps: Whisk the cornstarch, sugar, matcha, and water together in a pan until smooth.
Cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until thickened and glossy, about 8 minutes.
Pour into a tray, refrigerate for 1 hour, then cut into cubes and dust with matcha.

dishes.name: Herb Rice Pilaf
steps: Saute the onion in butter over medium heat until soft.
Add the rice and stir until the grains are coated and glossy.
Pour in the stock and bring to a boil, about 2 minutes.
Cover and simmer for 18 minutes, until the liquid is absorbed.
Fluff with a fork and fold in the parsley.

rice_cooker: Saute the onion in butter over medium heat until soft.
Tip the onion, rice, stock, thyme, and salt into the rice cooker and cook until it clicks off, about 25 minutes.
Fluff with a fork and fold in the parsley.

dishes.name: Roasted Potatoes
steps: Cut the potatoes into chunks and toss with olive oil, garlic, rosemary, salt, and pepper.
Roast at 200 C for 35 minutes, turning once, until golden and crisp.

air_fryer: Cut the potatoes into chunks and toss with olive oil, garlic, rosemary, salt, and pepper.
Air-fry at 200 C for 20 minutes, shaking the basket halfway, until golden and crisp.

dishes.name: Sukiyaki
steps: Make the warishita: combine the soy sauce, mirin, sugar and dashi, and stir until the sugar dissolves.
Heat a shallow pot and oil it lightly.
Sear a few beef slices briefly on one side to catch some colour.
Pour in the warishita and bring to a gentle simmer.
Add the napa cabbage and shirataki at one side of the pot and simmer for 6 minutes.
Add the tofu and enoki and simmer for 4 minutes more.
Add the remaining beef slices and cook for 1 minute, just until they lose their pink.
Scatter the scallion over and eat straight from the pot.
dishes.handson_action: tending the pot at the table

dishes.name: Shabu-Shabu
steps: Wipe the kombu and steep it in the water for 20 minutes.
Bring the pot to a bare simmer and lift out the kombu before it boils.
Season the broth lightly with salt.
Add the carrot and simmer gently for 10 minutes.
Add the napa cabbage, tofu and enoki and simmer for 4 minutes.
Swish each beef slice through the broth for a few seconds, until it just turns colour, and eat as you go.
dishes.handson_action: cooking each piece at the table

dishes.name: Milky Pork Bone Broth
steps: Blanch the pork bones in boiling water for 5 minutes, then drain and rinse away the scum.
Return the bones to a clean pot with the water, onion, garlic and ginger.
Bring to a rolling boil and keep it boiling hard for 3 hours, topping up the water as it drops.
The hard boil is the point: it emulsifies the fat and collagen into the water and turns the broth opaque and milky.
Strain, season with salt, and finish with scallion.

dishes.name: Tom Yum Goong
steps: Bruise the lemongrass and slice the galangal.
Bring the water to a simmer with the lemongrass, galangal and kaffir lime leaves and steep for 8 minutes.
Add the mushroom and tomato and simmer for 4 minutes.
Add the prawns and cook for 3 minutes, until they curl and turn opaque.
Take the pot off the heat.
Season off the heat with fish sauce, lime juice and sugar, so the lime stays sharp.
Add the chilli to taste.
dishes.handson_action: balancing the sour and the heat

dishes.name: Teochew Bak Kut Teh
steps: Blanch the pork ribs in boiling water for 3 minutes, then drain.
Lightly crush the whole garlic bulbs, skin and all.
Crack the white peppercorns coarsely.
Put the ribs, garlic and peppercorns in a pot with the water.
Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer gently for 90 minutes, skimming as it goes.
Season with salt. The broth should stay clear and taste of pepper and garlic — nothing else.

dishes.name: Laksa Lemak
steps: Fry the laksa paste in the oil over medium heat for 4 minutes, until it darkens and smells fragrant.
Pour in the water and bring to a simmer.
Stir in the coconut milk and simmer gently for 10 minutes — do not let it boil hard or it will split.
Add the fish cake and simmer for 3 minutes.
Add the prawns and cook for 3 minutes.
Blanch the noodles and beansprouts separately and put them in the bowls.
Ladle the gravy over and finish with torn laksa leaf.

dishes.name: Sundubu Jjigae
steps: Fry the pork belly in the sesame oil until the fat renders.
Add the kimchi, garlic and onion and fry for 3 minutes.
Stir in the gochugaru and gochujang and fry for 1 minute, until the oil turns red.
Pour in the water and bring to a boil.
Simmer for 10 minutes.
Spoon in the soft tofu in large curds and simmer for 3 minutes.
Crack the egg into the middle, scatter the scallion, and serve bubbling.

dishes.name: Century Egg Congee
steps: Rinse the rice and stir a little oil and salt through it — it helps the grains break down.
Bring the water to a boil with the ginger.
Add the rice, lower the heat, and simmer gently for 60 minutes, stirring now and then so it does not catch.
The rice should collapse into a thick, silky porridge.
Add the chicken and simmer for 12 minutes.
Dice the century eggs and stir them through for the last 3 minutes.
Season with salt, pepper and sesame oil, and finish with scallion.

dishes.name: Teochew Steamed Pomfret
steps: Scale and gut the pomfret, then score it twice on each side so the heat gets in.
Lay the ginger under and over the fish on a heatproof plate.
Scatter the tomato, salted vegetable and soaked shiitake around it.
Sprinkle over the shaoxing wine and soy sauce.
Steam over rapidly boiling water for 10 minutes — the flesh should just flake at the bone.
Take it off the heat the moment it is done; a minute too long and it turns woolly.
Finish with scallion and a spoonful of hot sesame oil.

dishes.name: Fish Head Curry
steps: Pound the shallots, garlic, ginger and lemongrass to a rough paste.
Fry the paste in oil over medium heat for 5 minutes until it darkens and smells fragrant.
Stir in the curry powder and curry leaves and fry for 1 minute.
Add the water and tamarind and bring to a simmer.
Add the eggplant and okra and simmer for 8 minutes.
Stir in the coconut milk and simmer gently — do not let it boil hard or it will split.
Slide in the fish head and tomato and simmer for 12 minutes, spooning the gravy over.
Season with salt and rest it for a few minutes before serving.

dishes.name: Sambal Grilled Mackerel
steps: Pat the mackerel dry and score the thickest part so it cooks evenly.
Mix the sambal with the tamarind and a little oil.
Spread the sambal over both sides of the fish and leave it for 15 minutes.
Grill over high heat for 6 minutes on the first side.
Turn once and grill for 4 minutes more, until the skin chars and the flesh flakes.
Squeeze the lime over and serve straight away.

dishes.name: Stir-Fried Beansprouts with Salted Fish
steps: Rinse the beansprouts and pick off the straggly tails if you can be bothered.
Fry the diced salted fish in oil over medium heat until it is crisp and smells strong, then lift it out.
Turn the heat to high and fry the garlic and chilli for 20 seconds.
Throw in the beansprouts and toss hard for 90 seconds — they should stay squeaky, not go limp.
Return the salted fish, season with soy, and take it straight off the heat.

dishes.name: Braised Napa Cabbage with Dried Shrimp
steps: Soak the dried shrimp and shiitake in warm water for 15 minutes, then slice the mushrooms. Keep the soaking water.
Fry the garlic and dried shrimp in oil until fragrant.
Add the cabbage and toss until it begins to wilt.
Pour in the mushroom water and oyster sauce, cover, and simmer for 12 minutes until the cabbage is soft and sweet.
Finish with sesame oil.

dishes.name: Sambal Okra
steps: Trim the okra but leave them whole — cutting them makes them slimy.
Blanch the okra for 2 minutes and drain well.
Pound the shallots and dried shrimp to a rough paste.
Fry the paste and sambal in oil for 4 minutes until the oil separates and it smells sweet.
Stir in the tamarind and sugar.
Fold the okra through the sambal for 1 minute, just to coat.
Season and serve.

dishes.name: Chye Sim with Oyster Sauce
steps: Bring a pot of water to a rolling boil with a splash of oil and a pinch of salt.
Blanch the chye sim for 60 seconds — it should still have a snap.
Drain it well and lay it out on the plate.
Fry the sliced garlic in oil until it just turns gold.
Stir the oyster sauce, sugar and sesame oil into the hot garlic oil.
Pour the sauce over the greens and serve at once.

dishes.name: Steamed Eggplant with Garlic and Soy
steps: Cut the eggplant into long batons and lay them on a heatproof plate.
Steam for 10 minutes, until a chopstick slides through with no resistance.
Tip away the water that collects on the plate — it will make the dish watery.
Fry the minced garlic and chilli in oil over low heat until pale gold; do not let it brown.
Stir in the soy, sugar and sesame oil.
Pour the garlic dressing over the eggplant and scatter the scallion.

dishes.name: Watercress and Wolfberry Soup
steps: Wash the watercress well — grit hides in the stems.
Bring the water to a boil with the ginger and soaked shiitake.
Add the watercress and simmer gently for 20 minutes, until it collapses and the broth turns green and sweet.
Add the wolfberries for the last 5 minutes.
Season with salt.

dishes.name: Braised Daikon with Mushroom
steps: Peel the daikon and cut it into thick rounds.
Soak the shiitake and slice them, keeping the water.
Fry the garlic and ginger in oil until fragrant.
Add the daikon and mushrooms and toss for 2 minutes.
Pour in the mushroom water and the sauces, cover, and simmer for 30 minutes until the daikon is translucent and soft.
Finish with scallion.

dishes.name: Stir-Fried Pumpkin with Egg
steps: Peel the pumpkin and slice it thin so it cooks fast.
Fry the garlic in oil until fragrant.
Add the pumpkin and toss, then add the water, cover, and let it steam for 5 minutes until just tender.
Push the pumpkin aside, pour in the beaten egg, and let it set before folding it through.
Season with salt and white pepper and finish with scallion.

dishes.name: Sayur Lodeh
steps: Fry the laksa paste and bruised lemongrass in oil for 4 minutes until it darkens.
Pour in the water and bring to a simmer.
Add the carrot and simmer for 8 minutes.
Add the cabbage, eggplant and long beans and simmer for 6 minutes.
Stir in the coconut milk and the tofu and simmer gently for 5 minutes — do not boil it hard or it will split.
Season with salt.

dishes.name: Chap Chye
steps: Soak the shiitake and dried shrimp; keep the water.
Mash the fermented beancurd with a little of the soaking water.
Fry the garlic and dried shrimp in oil until fragrant.
Stir in the fermented beancurd and fry for 1 minute — this is the backbone of the dish.
Add the cabbage, mushrooms and shirataki, then the soaking water and oyster sauce.
Cover and simmer for 20 minutes, until the cabbage is meltingly soft.
Add the tofu for the last 5 minutes.
